name: Quantum Confinement in Nanocrystals
description: When a semiconductor crystal shrinks below the exciton Bohr radius, charge carriers become spatially confined, their energy levels become discrete, and the band gap widens with decreasing size; this size-tunable gap shifts the absorption and emission, allowing the synthesis size to be adjusted to hit a target wavelength.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Bulk semiconductor"]
    N2["Reduce size toward nm"]
    N3{"Size < exciton Bohr radius?"}
    N4["Bulk-like band gap"]
    N5["Confine carriers"]
    N6["Discrete energy levels"]
    N7["Band gap widens"]
    N8["Size-tunable emission"]
    N9{"Hit target wavelength?"}
    N10["Adjust synthesis size"]
    N11["Quantum dot, target gap"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N3 -->|no| N4
    N4 -->|shrink more| N2
    N3 -->|yes| N5
    N5 --> N6
    N6 --> N7
    N7 --> N8
    N8 --> N9
    N9 -->|no| N10
    N10 -->|re-grow| N2
    N9 -->|yes| N11

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N3 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N4 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N5 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N6 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff

sources.title: Electron-electron and electron-hole interactions in small semiconductor crystallites
Semiconductor clusters, nanocrystals, and quantum dots
